reasoning: The probability of a formally ratified peace treaty being completed before the end of 2025 is extremely low. There are only ~38 days remaining in the year. Although US President Trump is pushing a 28-point peace plan and gave Ukraine a one-week ultimatum in late November 2025, a YES resolution strictly requires a 'formally ratified peace treaty,' not just an armistice or framework agreement. Ukrainian leadership has publicly resisted the plan, warning that it would damage Ukraine's dignity and freedom. Furthermore, the plan requires territorial concessions. Under Article 73 of the Ukrainian Constitution, altering Ukraine's territory requires an All-Ukrainian referendum, which is constitutionally prohibited while martial law is in effect. Even if Ukraine eventually capitulated to US pressure, drafting a comprehensive formal treaty and passing the necessary constitutional hurdles or referendums for ratification within a 5-week window is practically impossible. Given these immense political, legal, and temporal constraints, the probability is at the 2% floor.
